title: Tampa Immigration Lawyers | Brown Immigration Law
content:
Brown Immigration Law provides experienced legal counsel to individuals, families, and businesses across Tampa, FL, from a team of immigration lawyers serving the Tampa Bay region. Our immigration law firm handles a wide range of cases, including visa petitions, green card applications, deportation defense, and family reunification, and our attorneys have the knowledge and resources to handle immigration matters of every size and complexity. With decades of combined experience, our firm works with clients throughout Hillsborough County and the broader Tampa Bay area to achieve lasting results in immigration law.

## Immigration Services for Families and Individuals in Tampa, FL
Tampa is home to diverse communities with strong ties to Latin America, the Caribbean, and beyond. Brown Immigration Law is committed to supporting families and individuals through every stage of the immigration process. Our services include:
- Permanent residence (green cards) : Assisting individuals who qualify for employment-based, humanitarian, or other permanent residence pathways beyond family sponsorship. We also help green card holders understand their rights and benefits as lawful permanent residents, including the ability to live and work permanently in the United States and pursue citizenship.
- Family-based visas : Assisting U.S. citizens and lawful permanent residents with K-1 visa applications , as well as with petitions for spouses, children, and parents .
- Student visas : Guiding international students through F-1 and other student visa applications and helping maintain status while studying in the United States.
- Naturalization and citizenship : Guiding lawful permanent residents through the steps required to become U.S. citizens.
- Humanitarian protections : Representing asylum seekers , Temporary Protected Status (TPS) applicants, and those applying for other humanitarian relief programs, including VAWA and U visa applications .
- Deportation and removal defense : Advocating for clients in proceedings before the Tampa immigration court and the Board of Immigration Appeals.
Whether you are bringing a loved one to the United States, looking to further your academic studies, or defending against deportation, our immigration attorneys in Tampa provide clear guidance and effective strategies tailored to your circumstances.

## Employment and Business Immigration in Tampa, Florida
Tampa’s economy thrives on a mix of international talent, immigrant entrepreneurs, and industries that rely on a strong and steady workforce. Brown Immigration Law partners with businesses of all sizes to provide immigration solutions that keep companies growing while ensuring compliance with federal requirements.
We assist Tampa employers and professionals with:
- Temporary work visas : Including H-1B visas for specialty occupations in technology and engineering, L-1 visas for intracompany transfers , O-1 visas for individuals of extraordinary ability, and TN for Canadian and Mexican professionals. We also advise business visitors on B-1/B-2 visas , ensuring short-term travel for meetings, training, or conferences is handled properly.
- Employment-based permanent residence : Sponsorship of workers for green cards in key industries such as healthcare, construction, and hospitality.
- Support for immigrant entrepreneurs and investors : Guiding business owners through options such as the EB-5 immigrant investor visa and other pathways that allow them to establish and expand companies in Florida.
- Workforce-driven industries : Advising employers in Tampa’s tourism, hospitality, and construction sectors on visa options, compliance, and planning for seasonal or high-volume hiring.
- Employer compliance : Helping companies avoid penalties by preparing for I-9 audits and implementing best practices.
Working with an immigration lawyer can help ensure that your documentation is in order and that your applications are filled out correctly, reducing the risk of rejection due to minor errors or insufficient documentation. Immigration officials can reject applications for various reasons, including minor errors on forms or insufficient documentation.
Immigrants power some of Tampa’s most important industries, from hotels and restaurants serving millions of visitors each year to tech startups in downtown and the Channelside District. By helping businesses secure visas and stay compliant, Brown Immigration Law ensures that Tampa companies remain competitive in a global economy.

## Why Immigration Law Matters in Tampa, FL
Tampa’s immigration landscape has its own character. Families and employers here face considerations that are different from those in Miami or Orlando. For example:
- Cultural communities : Tampa has one of the largest Cuban-American populations in the U.S. outside Miami, as well as long-established Puerto Rican, Mexican, and Colombian neighborhoods. Immigration issues often involve multi-generational families with complex histories of entry and status.
- Entry points : Tampa International Airport and Port Tampa Bay make the city a frequent arrival hub, whether for family visits, business travel, or shipping-related employment. That means more cases tied to overstays or port-of-entry inspections.
- Local industries : The Tampa Bay economy depends heavily on hospitality, healthcare, construction, and logistics. Immigration directly impacts the availability of workers and the ability of employers to meet demand.
- Historic role : From Ybor City’s immigrant-built cigar industry to today’s diverse workforce, immigration has always been central to Tampa’s growth.
Understanding these local factors is critical. Brown Immigration Law’s Tampa office doesn’t just apply federal law; we account for the cultural, economic, and practical realities of immigration in this region.

## Immigration Law Matters Unique to Tampa
While immigration law is federal, living in Tampa often creates circumstances that shape how cases unfold. Families and employers here face considerations that are different from those in other cities. These can include:
- Visa overstays through major travel hubs : Tampa International Airport and Port Tampa Bay make the city a frequent arrival hub, whether for family visits, business travel, or shipping-related employment. That means more cases tied to overstays or port-of-entry inspections. Even short overstays can complicate future applications.
- Seasonal and hospitality workers : Tampa’s tourism-driven economy creates demand for temporary and seasonal labor, leading to frequent issues with H-2B visas and employer compliance.
- Construction and trade industries : Ongoing growth in the Tampa Bay area has fueled demand for workers, but securing legal status for employees often requires careful immigration planning.
- Technology and healthcare sectors : Employers in Tampa increasingly depend on H-1B visas and other employment-based pathways to attract specialized talent.
- Petitioning under the Cuban Adjustment Act : Tampa has one of the largest Cuban-American populations in the U.S. outside Miami. While Tampa’s Cuban community benefits from unique legal provisions, the current climate could lead to changes in the law.
- Asylum and humanitarian claims : Florida’s role as a first point of arrival means many applicants must prepare strong cases on tight deadlines.
These issues require more than general immigration knowledge; they demand attorneys who understand how rapidly-changing federal law interacts with the economic and cultural realities of our community. Our team of Tampa immigration attorneys brings years of experience serving the area, providing clients with legal support tailored to the region’s unique needs.

## Why Choose Brown Immigration Law in Tampa, Florida
When you work with Brown Immigration Law, you benefit from a team that has handled thousands of immigration matters nationwide while maintaining a strong local presence in Tampa. Clients choose us because:
- We represent both individuals and corporate clients, giving us a wide perspective on immigration issues.
- Our immigration attorneys are experienced in complex cases, including inadmissibility waivers and deportation defense.
- We maintain a national reach with local insight, allowing us to serve Tampa clients while staying on top of developments in federal law.
- Our commitment to client service means providing personalized strategies and clear communication at every stage of the process.
- Our immigration practice covers the full spectrum of cases — from family reunification to asylum to employer compliance — and our Tampa office provides the same individualized attention that has earned Brown Immigration Law a national reputation for results.
Federal law is constantly changing. Our immigration lawyers in Tampa stay informed and adapt quickly, ensuring that clients receive reliable and effective representation.

## What to Bring to Your Consultation With a Tampa Immigration Attorney
A consultation with a Tampa immigration attorney is the ideal opportunity to discuss your needs and get answers to any questions you have. Come prepared with any documentation and evidence you have that could support your efforts. At the very least, bring:
- Passports (current and expired)
- Visa paperwork , approval notices, and prior applications
- Immigration court or USCIS correspondence
- Marriage, birth, or divorce certificates if applicable
- Employment records or business ownership documents for work-related cases
We also encourage you to bring a list of objectives, such as your expected needs, the desired timeline, and your long-term goals for bringing family to the U.S. Being organized ensures our attorneys can evaluate your situation quickly and provide accurate guidance from the start.

## Do You Have an Immigration Lawyer Near Me?
Our Tampa office is located at 400 N. Ashley Drive, Suite 1900, Tampa, FL 33602, in the heart of downtown along the Hillsborough River. We’re just steps from the Tampa Riverwalk and a short drive from I-275, Kennedy Boulevard, and the University of Tampa. Convenient parking is available in the building’s attached garage, with additional public parking lots nearby.
Brown Immigration Law assists clients in Hillsborough County and across the region, including nearby Pinellas, Pasco, and Polk counties. Whether you are attending a hearing at the local immigration court or preparing a petition through U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services (USCIS), our attorneys are positioned to provide guidance close to home.

## Abogados de Inmigración en Tampa Que Atienden en Español
Brown Immigration Law ofrece atención legal en español para personas y empresas en Tampa que necesitan orientación en temas migratorios. Nuestro equipo trabaja con familias, profesionales y empleadores en asuntos como residencia permanente, procesos de ciudadanía, visas de empleo y representación en casos de deportación, siempre con un enfoque práctico y claro.

## Speak With a Tampa, FL Immigration Attorney Today
If you need an immigration lawyer in Tampa, FL, Brown Immigration Law is ready to help. Whether your case involves family reunification, employment sponsorship, or removal defense, our attorneys have the skill and experience to guide you through the process.
Contact our Tampa office today to schedule a consultation with a knowledgeable immigration attorney. We are here to serve families, individuals, and employers throughout Tampa, Hillsborough County, and the Tampa Bay region. We maintain another office in Orlando and additional offices in Durham, NC , Columbus, OH , and Cleveland, OH .

## Immigration in Tampa, Florida: Frequently Asked Questions

## How do I choose the right immigration lawyer in Tampa, FL?
Choosing the right immigration lawyer in Tampa starts with finding an attorney who handles both family- and employment-based immigration matters and understands local immigration courts and USCIS procedures. Experience with cases involving Tampa families, workers, and employers is an important factor when evaluating legal representation.

## How much does a Tampa immigration lawyer cost?
The cost of working with a Tampa immigration lawyer depends on several factors, including the type of case, your current immigration status, and the complexity of the process involved. During a consultation, an immigration attorney can explain expected fees and provide clear information about pricing before any work begins.

## What does an immigration attorney in Tampa help with?
An immigration attorney in Tampa can assist with a wide range of matters, including family-based petitions, employment visas, permanent residence applications, citizenship and naturalization, and defense in removal proceedings. Attorneys also help ensure filings are accurate and submitted on time to avoid unnecessary delays.

## Can Brown Immigration Law help with visas for Tampa businesses?
Yes. Brown Immigration Law works with employers throughout Tampa who need assistance from a visa lawyer for work authorization and sponsorship matters. We handle temporary work visas, employment-based green cards, and ongoing compliance issues for businesses across multiple industries.

## Do I need an immigration lawyer near me to handle my case?
Many people search for an immigration lawyer near me when they need local guidance and accessibility. Working with a Tampa-based immigration lawyer allows clients to meet in person when needed and ensures familiarity with the local immigration court and regional USCIS offices.

## Does Brown Immigration Law represent clients outside Hillsborough County?
Yes. While our office is located in Tampa, our immigration attorneys represent clients throughout the Tampa Bay region, including Pinellas, Pasco, and Polk counties, as well as individuals and businesses across Florida.

## What immigration options exist for Cuban or Puerto Rican families in Tampa?
Cuban nationals in Tampa may qualify for adjustment of status under the Cuban Adjustment Act, while Puerto Rican families have unique considerations related to U.S. citizenship. An experienced Tampa immigration attorney from our firm can review your situation and explain which options may apply.

## Can a Tampa green card lawyer help with permanent residence applications?
A Tampa green card lawyer from Brown Immigration Law can assist individuals and employers with permanent residence applications through family-based, employment-based, and other qualifying immigration pathways. Legal guidance is especially helpful when applications involve prior immigration history or complex documentation requirements.

## ¿Atienden a personas que buscan un abogado de inmigración en Tampa?
Sí. Brown Immigration Law brinda asesoría legal en español a personas y empresas en Tampa que necesitan ayuda con procesos migratorios, incluyendo residencia permanente, visas de trabajo y asuntos relacionados con su estatus migratorio.
